repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Closes #39432.

### TLDR

Adds to `vim.ui.img.Opts` two fields:

1. `relative` that can be set to `ui`, `editor`, or `buffer`
2. `buf` that specifies the buffer when using `relative=buffer`

I've also got a third that I'm less convinced in, which is `pad` to do padding for buffer-local images.

*NOTE: I've got the commit from #39484 on this PR because I built on top of it. So it needs to be finished first for me to rebase.*

### About the changes

Builds on top of #39484, adding a `relative` option to `vim.ui.img.Opts` so we can distinguish between three modes:

1. `ui`: what we have thus far, but to my knowledge the position is relative to the terminal and not necessarily the editor, so in certain circumstances this may show an image outside of neovim
4. `editor`: displays images in locations relative to neovim by creating a floating window and leveraging unicode characters to map an image on top
5. `buffer`: displays images inside a buffer by making use of virtual lines and extmarks to embed unicode characters to map an image

To get unicode placements to work, I also needed to be able to get the width and height of a cell as pixels so I could translate the width and height of a PNG into cells, which I only need to use when a width and/or height is not provided when displaying an image via `editor` or `buffer` since I need to know how big to make the virtual space for unicode placeholders.

The two tty functions - and I expect we'd want a conversation about whether these should be refactored now into a unified query function - are:

1, `query_csi(payload, opts, cb)`: like `query_apc()` but for CSI responses (used for CSI 16t)
2. `cell_px()`: queries terminal cell size in pixels via CSI 16t, cached after first call

### Testing & Quirks

Note that I've tested this with all three options (`ui`, `editor`, `buffer`) and it works fine on ghostty and kitty. I realized that WezTerm doesn't work as it seems to glitch out with images. The `ui` approach works on WezTerm. Placeholders does not. cc @wez

Separately, for the floating window with placeholders used to render the image, I haven't been able to figure out if I can get transparency to work. A bit stuck here as blend modes don't seem to help.

Here's a video from ghostty showing some images placed within a buffer, and also the `editor` mode following my mouse:


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08446c6d-06e8-420e-9da5-9a0d63ae7c10

comment_data.body: i will try to wrap up https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39489 soon
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -139,4 +144,74 @@ function M.query_apc(payload, opts, cb)
   end)
 end
 
+--- Send a CSI sequence to the terminal and call {cb} for each CSI response received.
+--- Cleans up after {timeout} milliseconds if no response is received.
+---
+--- {cb} receives the full CSI sequence including the `\027[` prefix.
+--- Return `true` from {cb} to stop listening.
+---
+---@param payload string CSI sequence to send (full escape sequence including prefix)
+---@param opts {timeout?:integer} Options table (timeout in milliseconds, default 1000)
+---@param cb fun(resp:string):boolean? Callback invoked for each CSI TermResponse
+---@overload fun(payload:string, cb:fun(resp:string):boolean?)
+function M.query_csi(payload, opts, cb)

comment_data.body: not necessary to mention news for features that are already in news :)

 • `vim.ui.img` experimental module added to display images within neovim.
 • `:checkhealth vim.ui.img` reports terminal graphics protocol support.
 • `vim.ui.img.del(math.huge)` clears all images.
+• `vim.ui.img` supports three placement modes via `Opts.relative`:

@@ -19,6 +24,39 @@ local generate_id = (function()
   end
 end)()
 
+---@alias vim.ui.img._kitty.State
+---|{ ui: true }                      -- ui mode: terminal-native kitty placement
+---|{ win: integer, buf: integer }    -- editor mode: floating window + scratch buffer
+---|{ mark: integer, buf: integer }   -- buffer mode: extmark ID + user buffer handle
+

+---@type table<integer, vim.ui.img._kitty.State>
+local state = {}
+
+local NAMESPACE = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace('vim.ui.img.kitty')

comment_data.body: lowercase. besides the fact that this is not actually constant (caller can easily mutate it), it's just not useful. if we want actually immutability it should be a function, which... is also lowercase
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+-- Diacritics for the kitty graphics protocol unicode-placeholder mode.
+-- Each image cell is U+10EEEE followed by a row diacritic and a column diacritic,
+-- with the image ID encoded in the cell's foreground color. Diacritics are taken
+-- from kitty's rowcolumn-diacritics.txt (Unicode combining class 230, no canonical
+-- decomposition); DIACRITICS[i] encodes value i-1.
+--
+-- Row and column diacritics are emitted explicitly on every cell rather than using
+-- the protocol's inheritance rule, because inheritance breaks under horizontal
+-- scrolling: if the viewport shifts right, cells at the left edge have no left
+-- neighbor and the terminal cannot recover their column position.
+---@class vim.ui.img._placeholders
+local M = {}
+
+---@type table<integer, integer>
+local DIACRITIC_CODEPOINTS = {

comment_data.body: maybe this should be a separate PR? UI-owned placement seems like extra scope and complicates this initial phase. If we want to introduce that we probably also should think about how it will look in the ui protocol.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -113,34 +168,218 @@ local function place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
   )
 end
 
----Transmit image bytes and place the image. Returns both IDs.
+---Build virt_lines of placeholder chars for nvim_buf_set_extmark (buffer mode).
+---Each cell: U+10EEEE + row diacritic + col diacritic, colored with img_id highlight.
+---@param img_id integer
+---@param width integer
+---@param height integer
+---@param pad integer leading blank cells per row
+---@return {[1]:string, [2]:string}[][] virt_lines
+local function build_placeholder_lines(img_id, width, height, pad)
+  local hl = img_hl(img_id)
+
+  ---@type {[1]:string, [2]:string}[][]
+  local lines = {}
+  for r = 0, height - 1 do
+    -- Build our row of placeholders representing the positions of the image to display
+    local row_str = ''
+    for c = 0, width - 1 do
+      row_str = row_str .. PLACEHOLDER .. DIACRITICS[r + 1] .. DIACRITICS[c + 1]
+    end
+
+    -- Build our line, optionally including padding
+    ---@type {[1]:string, [2]:string}[]
+    local line = {}
+    if pad > 0 then
+      line[#line + 1] = { string.rep(' ', pad), 'Normal' }
+    end
+    line[#line + 1] = { row_str, hl }
+
+    lines[#lines + 1] = line
+  end
+
+  return lines
+end
+
+---Build buffer lines of placeholder chars for a scratch buffer (editor mode).
+---Returns plain strings suitable for nvim_buf_set_lines.
+---@param width integer
+---@param height integer
+---@return string[] lines
+local function build_scratch_lines(width, height)
+  ---@type string[]
+  local lines = {}
+
+  for r = 0, height - 1 do
+    local row_str = ''
+    for c = 0, width - 1 do
+      row_str = row_str .. PLACEHOLDER .. DIACRITICS[r + 1] .. DIACRITICS[c + 1]
+    end
+    lines[r + 1] = row_str
+  end
+
+  return lines
+end
+
+---Transmit image bytes and set up display (floating window or extmark).
+---Returns img_id and placement_id.
 ---@param data string raw image bytes
 ---@param opts vim.ui.img.Opts
 ---@return integer img_id
 ---@return integer placement_id
 function M.set(data, opts)
+  local relative = opts.relative or (opts.buf ~= nil and 'buffer' or 'ui')
+
+  -- editor/buffer modes require explicit cell dimensions for the unicode placeholder grid;
+  -- ui mode delegates sizing to kitty so width/height are optional.
+  if relative ~= 'ui' and (not opts.width or not opts.height) then
+    local px_w, px_h = png_dimensions(data)
+    if px_w then
+      local cell = require('vim.tty').cell_px()
+      opts.width  = opts.width  or math.ceil(px_w / cell[1])
+      opts.height = opts.height or math.ceil(px_h / cell[2])
+    end
+  end
+  assert(relative == 'ui' or (opts.width and opts.height), 'width and height required (could not derive from image data)')
+
+  -- Upload our image data, which we'll use for future placements
   local img_id = generate_id()
   local placement_id = generate_id()
-
   transmit(img_id, data)
-  place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+
+  -- Placement method depends on relative:
+  --
+  -- 1. 'ui': terminal-native kitty placement via cursor save/move/restore

comment_data.body:
What we did in the original PR for absolute is the `ui` version, so I was keeping it around. I don't feel like it adds much more complexity or scope given that we're keeping what is currently in neovim now (just calling it relative='ui'). The newer stuff (relative='editor' and relative='buffer') is what adds more complexity, and is what this PR is adding as net-new.

Are you okay with us actually keeping this? It'd be easier for me in a few areas to keep this in now versus needing to come back to it later.

+---@param width integer

+local function build_scratch_lines(width, height)
+  ---@type string[]

+  for r = 0, height - 1 do
+    local row_str = ''

 ---@param data string raw image bytes
 ---@param opts vim.ui.img.Opts
 ---@return integer img_id
 ---@return integer placement_id
 function M.set(data, opts)
+  local relative = opts.relative or (opts.buf ~= nil and 'buffer' or 'ui')
+
+  -- editor/buffer modes require explicit cell dimensions for the unicode placeholder grid;
+  -- ui mode delegates sizing to kitty so width/height are optional.
+  if relative ~= 'ui' and (not opts.width or not opts.height) then
+    local px_w, px_h = png_dimensions(data)
+    if px_w then
+      local cell = require('vim.tty').cell_px()
+      opts.width  = opts.width  or math.ceil(px_w / cell[1])
+      opts.height = opts.height or math.ceil(px_h / cell[2])
+    end
+  end
+  assert(relative == 'ui' or (opts.width and opts.height), 'width and height required (could not derive from image data)')
+
+  -- Upload our image data, which we'll use for future placements
   local img_id = generate_id()
   local placement_id = generate_id()
-
   transmit(img_id, data)
-  place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+
+  -- Placement method depends on relative:
+  --
+  -- 1. 'ui': terminal-native kitty placement via cursor save/move/restore

comment_data.body: yeah its fine, I'm mostly thinking about how to present this to users in docs, and UIs in the ui-protocol

comment_data.body: This is especially relevant for me as I can't figure out any way to have transparency work with the floating window approach of editor. If I could get it to support transparency like what you get with kitty's APIs without unicode, then I'd be fine removing UI for now. I think it's a limitation of the floating window's background, but could be wrong there.

that is exactly what we need to list so we understand what's missing from our existing mechanisms :)

btw, why is transparency needed?

comment_data.body:
> btw, why is transparency needed?

So many exciting reasons! 😄 

Being able to layer images on top of each other can be a pretty big deal, and even if you match the terminal's background color you won't be able to see images underneath the main one if you don't support transparency.

Overlays are a big one for me that you put near or on top of code while still being able to see the code underneath. Diagrams and schematics, like stuff from mermaid, are typically designed with transparent backgrounds so the boxy-ness (you can sort of see it with the floating window) can be a little jarring. 

For some folks, having a mismatched  background color not matching the terminal theme is a frustrating experience.

Doing nifty things like circular loading animations (just by swapping the image bytes using the luv timer).

+
+    -- Build our line, optionally including padding
+    ---@type {[1]:string, [2]:string}[]
+    local line = {}
+    if pad > 0 then
+      line[#line + 1] = { string.rep(' ', pad), 'Normal' }
+    end
+    line[#line + 1] = { row_str, hl }
+
+    lines[#lines + 1] = line
+  end
+
+  return lines
+end
+
+---Build buffer lines of placeholder chars for a scratch buffer (editor mode).
+---Returns plain strings suitable for nvim_buf_set_lines.
+---@param width integer
+---@param height integer
+---@return string[] lines
+local function build_scratch_lines(width, height)
+  ---@type string[]
+  local lines = {}
+
+  for r = 0, height - 1 do
+    local row_str = ''
+    for c = 0, width - 1 do
+      row_str = row_str .. PLACEHOLDER .. DIACRITICS[r + 1] .. DIACRITICS[c + 1]
+    end
+    lines[r + 1] = row_str
+  end
+
+  return lines
+end
+
+---Transmit image bytes and set up display (floating window or extmark).
+---Returns img_id and placement_id.
 ---@param data string raw image bytes
 ---@param opts vim.ui.img.Opts
 ---@return integer img_id
 ---@return integer placement_id
 function M.set(data, opts)
+  local relative = opts.relative or (opts.buf ~= nil and 'buffer' or 'ui')
+
+  -- editor/buffer modes require explicit cell dimensions for the unicode placeholder grid;
+  -- ui mode delegates sizing to kitty so width/height are optional.
+  if relative ~= 'ui' and (not opts.width or not opts.height) then
+    local px_w, px_h = png_dimensions(data)
+    if px_w then
+      local cell = require('vim.tty').cell_px()
+      opts.width  = opts.width  or math.ceil(px_w / cell[1])
+      opts.height = opts.height or math.ceil(px_h / cell[2])
+    end
+  end
+  assert(relative == 'ui' or (opts.width and opts.height), 'width and height required (could not derive from image data)')
+
+  -- Upload our image data, which we'll use for future placements
   local img_id = generate_id()
   local placement_id = generate_id()
-
   transmit(img_id, data)
-  place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+
+  -- Placement method depends on relative:
+  --
+  -- 1. 'ui': terminal-native kitty placement via cursor save/move/restore

comment_data.body:
this looks pretty clumsy, or at least I would expect this logic to live in the "generalized" layer, not in the backend-specific layer (`_kitty.lua`).

if every backend impl has to reimplement this, we'll surely end up with an inconsistent and bug-prone experience?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -113,34 +150,230 @@ local function place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
   )
 end
 
----Transmit image bytes and place the image. Returns both IDs.
+---Build virt_lines of placeholder chars for nvim_buf_set_extmark (buffer mode).
+---Each cell: U+10EEEE + row diacritic + col diacritic, colored with img_id highlight.
+---@param img_id integer
+---@param width integer
+---@param height integer
+---@param pad integer leading blank cells per row
+---@return {[1]:string, [2]:string}[][] virt_lines
+local function build_placeholder_lines(img_id, width, height, pad)
+  local hl = img_hl(img_id)
+
+  ---@type {[1]:string, [2]:string}[][]
+  local lines = {}
+  for r = 0, height - 1 do
+    -- Build our row of placeholders representing the positions of the image to display
+    local row_str = ''
+    for c = 0, width - 1 do
+      row_str = row_str .. util.cell_unicode(r, c)
+    end
+
+    -- Build our line, optionally including padding
+    ---@type {[1]:string, [2]:string}[]
+    local line = {}
+    if pad > 0 then
+      line[#line + 1] = { string.rep(' ', pad), 'Normal' }
+    end
+    line[#line + 1] = { row_str, hl }
+
+    lines[#lines + 1] = line
+  end
+
+  return lines
+end
+
+---Build buffer lines of placeholder chars for a scratch buffer (editor mode).
+---Returns plain strings suitable for nvim_buf_set_lines.
+---@param width integer
+---@param height integer
+---@return string[] lines
+local function build_scratch_lines(width, height)
+  ---@type string[]
+  local lines = {}
+
+  for r = 0, height - 1 do
+    local row_str = ''
+    for c = 0, width - 1 do
+      row_str = row_str .. util.cell_unicode(r, c)
+    end
+    lines[r + 1] = row_str
+  end
+
+  return lines
+end
+
+---Transmit image bytes and set up display (floating window or extmark).
+---Returns img_id and placement_id.
 ---@param data string raw image bytes
 ---@param opts vim.ui.img.Opts
 ---@return integer img_id
 ---@return integer placement_id
 function M.set(data, opts)
-  local img_id = generate_id()
-  local placement_id = generate_id()
+  local relative = opts.relative or (opts.buf ~= nil and 'buffer' or 'ui')
 
+  -- editor/buffer modes require explicit cell dimensions for the unicode placeholder grid;
+  -- ui mode delegates sizing to kitty so width/height are optional.
+  if relative ~= 'ui' and (not opts.width or not opts.height) then
+    local px_w, px_h = png_dimensions(data)
+    if px_w then
+      local width_px, height_px = util.cell_size_px()
+      opts.width = opts.width or math.ceil(px_w / width_px)
+      opts.height = opts.height or math.ceil(px_h / height_px)
+    end
+  end
+  assert(
+    relative == 'ui' or (opts.width and opts.height),
+    'width and height required (could not derive from image data)'
+  )
+  if relative ~= 'ui' then
+    util.assert_unicode_range(opts.width, opts.height)
+  end
+
+  -- Upload our image data, which we'll use for future placements
+  local img_id = util.generate_id()
+  local placement_id = util.generate_id()
   transmit(img_id, data)
-  place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+
+  -- Placement method depends on relative:
+  --
+  -- 1. 'ui': terminal-native kitty placement via cursor save/move/restore
+  -- 2. 'editor': frameless floating window filled with unicode placeholder chars
+  -- 3. 'buffer': extmark virt_lines filled with unicode placeholder chars
+  --
+  -- editor/buffer modes use an invisible virtual placement (U=1) so kitty maps
+  -- each placeholder cell to the correct image region via the image ID in fg color.
+  if relative == 'ui' then
+    place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+    state[img_id] = { ui = true }
+  elseif relative == 'editor' then
+    create_virtual_placement(img_id, placement_id, opts.width, opts.height)
+
+    -- The image ID is encoded in the foreground color
+    vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, img_hl(img_id), { fg = img_id })
+    local scratch_buf = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true)
+    local lines = build_scratch_lines(opts.width, opts.height)
+    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(scratch_buf, 0, -1, false, lines)
+    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(scratch_buf, ns_id, 0, 0, {
+      end_row = opts.height,
+      hl_group = img_hl(img_id),
+    })
+
+    local win = vim.api.nvim_open_win(scratch_buf, false, {
+      relative = 'editor',
+      row = (opts.row or 1) - 1,
+      col = (opts.col or 1) - 1,
+      width = opts.width,
+      height = opts.height,
+      style = 'minimal',
+      border = 'none',
+      focusable = false,
+      noautocmd = true,
+      zindex = opts.zindex,
+    })
+
+    state[img_id] = { win = win, buf = scratch_buf }
+  elseif relative == 'buffer' then
+    create_virtual_placement(img_id, placement_id, opts.width, opts.height)
+
+    -- The image ID is encoded in the foreground color
+    vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, img_hl(img_id), { fg = img_id })
+    local buf = opts.buf
+    if buf == nil or buf == 0 then
+      buf = vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
+    end
+    local row = (opts.row or 1) - 1
+    local col = (opts.col or 1) - 1
+    local pad = opts.pad or 0
+    local lines = build_placeholder_lines(img_id, opts.width, opts.height, pad)
+    local mark = vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(buf, ns_id, row, col, {
+      virt_lines = lines,
+      invalidate = true,
+    })
+
+    state[img_id] = { mark = mark, buf = buf }
+  end
 
   return img_id, placement_id
 end
 
 ---Update an existing placement (flicker-free, reuses same IDs).
 ---@param img_id integer
 ---@param placement_id integer
----@param opts vim.ui.img.Opts
+---@param opts vim.ui.img.Opts merged opts
 function M.update(img_id, placement_id, opts)
-  place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+  local entry = state[img_id]
+  if not entry then
+    return
+  end
+
+  if not entry.ui then
+    util.assert_unicode_range(opts.width or 0, opts.height or 0)
+  end
+
+  -- We check the type of placement method based on the state's information:
+  --
+  -- 1. ui = true: we need to update a kitty image, which is relative to the terminal ui
+  -- 2. win exists: the image is using a floating window with unicode placeholders
+  -- 3. otherwise: the image is within a buffer with unicode placeholders
+  local is_relative_ui = entry.ui
+  local is_relative_editor = entry.win
+
+  if is_relative_ui then
+    place(img_id, placement_id, opts)
+  elseif is_relative_editor then
+    create_virtual_placement(img_id, placement_id, opts.width, opts.height)
+
+    vim.api.nvim_win_set_config(entry.win, {
+      relative = 'editor',
+      row = (opts.row or 1) - 1,
+      col = (opts.col or 1) - 1,
+      width = opts.width,
+      height = opts.height,
+      zindex = opts.zindex,
+    })
+    local lines = build_scratch_lines(opts.width, opts.height)
+    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(entry.buf, 0, -1, false, lines)
+    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(entry.buf, ns_id, 0, 0, {
+      end_row = opts.height,
+      hl_group = img_hl(img_id),
+    })
+  else
+    create_virtual_placement(img_id, placement_id, opts.width, opts.height)
+
+    local pad = opts.pad or 0
+    local lines = build_placeholder_lines(img_id, opts.width, opts.height, pad)
+    local row = (opts.row or 1) - 1
+    local col = (opts.col or 1) - 1
+    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(entry.buf, ns_id, row, col, {
+      id = entry.mark,
+      virt_lines = lines,
+      invalidate = true,
+    })
+  end
 end
 
 ---Delete an image and all its placements from the terminal.
 ---When {img_id} is `math.huge`, deletes all images.
 ---@param img_id integer
 function M.delete(img_id)
   if img_id == math.huge then
+    for id, entry in pairs(state) do
+      if entry.win then
+        if vim.api.nvim_win_is_valid(entry.win) then
+          vim.api.nvim_win_close(entry.win, true)
+        end
+        if vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(entry.buf) then
+          vim.api.nvim_buf_delete(entry.buf, { force = true })
+        end
+        vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, img_hl(id), {})
+      elseif entry.mark then

comment_regular_data.body: @justinmk just saw your work in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39489. Seems like it would be good for that to land and me update this PR to use it. Alongside #39484 that I'm dependent on right now.

@justinmk I built https://github.com/chipsenkbeil/alt-img.nvim to see how well this API including the buffer and editor relative concepts here would translate. Took awhile, but was able to successfully get both iterm2 and sixel to work on all three fronts.

So this PR seems to have pretty good legs to stand on from my perspective in terms of others integrating it to support not only absolute (UI) graphics but also editor & buffer relative graphics. The concept you wanted of swapping out `vim.ui.img` per implementation works well for both iterm2 and sixel. No need to include them in core 😄

@justinmk I moved some of the code over to a `_util.lua` file (renamed placements file to merge it into that). I went back to uppercase variable names that are local within `_util.lua` to signify they're read-only, but if there's a better naming you want let me know.

Once we get this one merged when you're happy with it, I've got a potential implementation for the #39434 and can also work to clean up my branch for #32189 that's built on top of this one. 😄

Just testing this and there is a lua error for large `relative='editor'` / `relative='buffer'` placements whose size is larger than allowed by the finite diacritic table (298x298 cells). Should we at least catch this so that this line:
```lua
row_str = row_str .. placeholder .. diacritics[r + 1] .. diacritics[c + 1]
```
doesn't produce a lua error, or is this intended to be left to the caller?

> Just testing this and there is a lua error for large `relative='editor'` / `relative='buffer'` placements whose size is larger than allowed by the finite diacritic table (298x298 cells). Should we at least catch this so that this line:
> 
> ```lua
> row_str = row_str .. placeholder .. diacritics[r + 1] .. diacritics[c + 1]
> ```
> 
> doesn't produce a lua error, or is this intended to be left to the caller?

Good catch. I didn't think to add a guard there, so now I have. 

@kovidgoyal I grabbed a copy of the diacritics from your [rowcolumn-diacritics.txt](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/_downloads/f0a0de9ec8d9ff4456206db8e0814937/rowcolumn-diacritics.txt). Is this the upper limit of the cell dimensions we can represent explicitly?

On Thu, May 14, 2026 at 08:39:24PM -0700, Chip Senkbeil wrote:
> chipsenkbeil left a comment (neovim/neovim#39496)
>
> > Just testing this and there is a lua error for large `relative='editor'` / `relative='buffer'` placements whose size is larger than allowed by the finite diacritic table (298x298 cells). Should we at least catch this so that this line:
> >
> > ```lua
> > row_str = row_str .. placeholder .. diacritics[r + 1] .. diacritics[c + 1]
> > ```
> >
> > doesn't produce a lua error, or is this intended to be left to the caller?
>
> Good catch. I didn't think to add a guard there, so now I have.
>
> @kovidgoyal I grabbed a copy of the diacritics from your [rowcolumn-diacritics.txt](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/_downloads/f0a0de9ec8d9ff4456206db8e0814937/rowcolumn-diacritics.txt). Is this the upper limit of the cell dimensions we can represent explicitly?

Yes, but you dont actually need to specify row and column numbers for
anything but the left most cell in each column, unless you have
overlapping images. This is explained in the spec.

And the list is linked to from the spec you dont have to get it
from kitty source code.

> Yes, but you dont actually need to specify row and column numbers for
anything but the left most cell in each column, unless you have
overlapping images. This is explained in the spec.

Even if you can scroll the image left-to-right to have the left-most column out of view? Also, feels like this isn't hurting and opens the door for image overlap anyway.

---

Following back up, @justinmk is there something else you're waiting on for this PR? Anything you need me to do before a full review?

> @justinmk is there something else you're waiting on for this PR? Anything you need me to do before a full review?

Mostly I just haven't had time, but am reluctant to YOLO merge this. Of course, anyone else is free to give it a thoughtful review...

It looks like you are using extmarks + floats to place things, that is a good sign 👍 

One concern is that it looks like the kitty backend is managing extmarks/floats itself. I would expect that to be done at a higher layer (e.g. `img.lua`), I would not expect each backend to manage its own extmarks/floats. That does NOT mean a lot of over-abstraction or "dependency injection" is needed; feel free to pass things to the backend; it just means that the backends should not have to reimplement placement logic (maybe an internal shared function?).

> One concern is that it looks like the kitty backend is managing extmarks/floats itself. I would expect that to be done at a higher layer (e.g. img.lua), I would not expect each backend to manage its own extmarks/floats. That does NOT mean a lot of over-abstraction or "dependency injection" is needed; feel free to pass things to the backend; it just means that the backends should not have to reimplement placement logic (maybe an internal shared function?).

I'm fine moving it out of kitty. Given that external clients are recommended to overwrite `vim.ui.img` entirely, I'm thinking some sort of shared helper like you mentioned is the way to go such that other implementors can choose to leverage the extmarks & float management through the shared utility, but if they want to do something entirely themselves they aren't locked into it to be compatible. Will toss a new commit on top of what is here so it's easier to see what has changed. Maybe next weekend when I'm back from some work travel 😄

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

title: feat(img): relative images (editor & buffer) using unicode placeholder
